message:
chore: sync beads tracker and regenerate enrollment/simulate test vectors

- Update .beads/issues.jsonl with new issue entry
- Regenerate request_vectors.cbor for enrollment and simulate test
  suites with updated key hex values and request IDs to match current
  key derivation output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -270,6 +270,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1pa8","title":"TEST-CORE: Protocol + crypto no-mock unit suites","description":"# TEST-CORE: Protocol + crypto no-mock unit suites

## Goal
Provide real-component unit tests for protocol framing, canonical CBOR, and crypto verification without mocks.

## Scope
- fcp-core: ObjectId derivation, approvals/receipts, revocation freshness logic.
- fcp-protocol: FCPS/FCPC parsing, length/flag validation, replay protection.
- fcp-crypto: Ed25519, X25519, HPKE seal/unseal, HKDF key schedule.
- fcp-cbor: canonicalization and schema hash prefixes.

## Constraints
- Use actual implementations; no stub crypto or fake framing.
- Prefer golden vectors and property tests over mocks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New unit suites run with no mocks/fakes.
- Negative tests cover malformed frames, invalid signatures, and HPKE AAD mismatches.
- Coverage increases for protocol/crypto hot paths.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

All negative tests already present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1pa8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-32k7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1pa8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-3p99","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1pdk","title":"[IDEA-12.3] Log harness tests","description":"## Goal
Add unit tests for the log harness itself.

## Details
- Validate schema errors are surfaced cleanly.
- Ensure malformed JSON lines are handled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Harness tests pass deterministically.","notes":"Added LogCapture tests in fcp-testkit/tracing_config.rs (valid tracing JSONL + schema validation, invalid JSON/missing fields). cargo fmt/check ok; cargo check ok; cargo clippy ok. cargo test -p fcp-testkit fails due to preexisting build errors: fcp-manifest RateLimit missing pool_name (lib.rs:1338) and fcp-core ambiguous_glob_reexports warning.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1pjhh","title":"[FWC] Fold remaining real fcp capabilities into fwc and retire fcp-cli","description":"Goal: make fwc the only canonical CLI by folding in the remaining real fcp capabilities before deleting the fcp-cli crate. Scope includes parity audit, porting honest file- and host-backed commands (context parity already exists; audit verify/timeline, manifest fix, net explain, trace replay, supply-chain verify/report/install/package, and any other non-simulated real workflows), refusing legacy fake/simulated placeholder behaviors, deleting the fcp-cli crate, updating docs/scripts/tests, and re-verifying via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:21:58.156562Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:13:08.886294Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T04:13:08.885843Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1pjhh","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:31:14.595233Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1624,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1pjhh","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"VERIFIED: All fcp-cli commands ported to fwc. Audit (verify/timeline/matrix/gaps), bench, budget, capabilities, manifest (fix), net (explain), trace (replay), supply-chain (verify/report), package, install, doctor, new, connector (→ list/show/ops/schema), context, explain, policy, repair — all present in fwc main.rs dispatch. fcp-cli crate already has Cargo.toml removed (not in workspace, not buildable). Source files remain as reference but crate is non-functional. Effective retirement complete.","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:13:06Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1ppcl","title":"[perf] reduce full offline discovery catalog load wall time","description":"Profile-first optimization for the manifest-backed DiscoveryCatalog::load() path that dominates offline fwc list/search/show latency. Baseline on the March 26, 2026 artifact binary: list --offline --json averaged about 438 ms/run over 20 runs, unfiltered search github --offline --json about 461 ms/run, while guide was about 7.7 ms/run. The common single-connector fast path was already landed in HEAD at ffdf98bf, so this bead now targets full catalog loads directly. Current implementation parallelizes manifest parsing in crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs with a bounded worker count, preserves deterministic slug sorting, and leaves connector-filtered fast paths untouched. Remote cargo verification remains blocked by rch dependency preflight RCH-E326; local fallback was interrupted immediately.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CreamReef","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:38:40.870487Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:41:52.055061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","perf","readiness"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1qwo","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Dependency Updates Required

Closed Dependabot PRs:
- sigstore-trust-root 0.4.0 -> 0.6.3 (MAJOR: sigstore verification)
- jsonschema 0.29.1 -> 0.41.0 (MAJOR: JSON schema validation, significant API changes)
- wasmtime-wasi 29.0.1 -> 41.0.3 (MAJOR: WASM runtime, major version bump)
- wasmtime 29.0.1 -> 41.0.3 (MAJOR: WASM runtime, must match wasmtime-wasi)
- criterion 0.5.1 -> 0.8.2 (MAJOR: benchmark framework)
- 8 rust minor updates (grouped)

WARNING: Multiple MAJOR version bumps. wasmtime 29->41 and jsonschema 29->41 will almost certainly have breaking API changes. This requires careful review of changelogs and migration guides.

## Steps
1. Run cargo update
2. Review wasmtime migration guide (29 -> 41 is 12 major versions)
3. Review jsonschema 0.29 -> 0.41 changelog
4. Fix compilation errors from major bumps
5. Run: cargo check --all-targets && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
6. Run: cargo test

## Testing
- cargo test must pass
- cargo clippy must pass with -D warnings
- Verify WASM plugin loading still works after wasmtime upgrade
- Verify JSON schema validation still works after jsonschema upgrade
- CI must pass on push","notes":"2026-02-28 (MagentaHawk): Ran cargo update; Cargo.lock now contains 60 compatible-version bumps including chrono 0.4.44, jsonschema 0.42.2, wasmtime/wasmtime-wasi 41.0.4, rustls 0.23.37, serde_with 3.17.0. Attempted validation via rch exec cargo check/clippy with isolated CARGO_HOME/CARGO_TARGET_DIR, but rch remote workers fail sync (rsync mkdir permission denied; workers probe reports connection failures) and local fallback hangs at build-script startup (proc-macro2 build-script stuck in dyld_start). cargo fmt --check passed. Keeping issue in_progress pending successful check/clippy execution in a low-contention/working-rch window.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaHawk","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:20:22.678222489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T21:45:25.416162Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T21:45:25.415080Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1683,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1qwo","author":"jemanuel","text":"MistyPond: Verified dependency updates via rch. All core crates compile and tests pass:
- fcp-core: 3844 tests OK
- fcp-crypto: 479 tests OK
- fcp-protocol: 448 tests OK
- fcp-store: 741 tests OK
- fcp-raptorq: 721 tests OK
- fcp-sdk: 9 tracing + existing tests OK
- fcp-host: compiles OK
- fcp-mesh, fcp-streaming, fcp-audit, fcp-webhook: compile OK
Workspace-wide check blocked by pre-existing asupersync BrowserHostApiIoCap error on some workers — not related to dependency updates.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:45:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1qwu","title":"[IDEA-18.1] Implement policy simulation harness + CLI","description":"## What
Implement a `fcp policy simulate` command and a reusable test harness API.

## How
- Load relevant mesh objects (ZonePolicy, CapabilityToken, RevocationHead, ApprovalTokens).
- Evaluate using policy engine; emit DecisionReceipt JSON.
- Ensure no write paths are exercised.

## Success Criteria
- CLI accepts a JSON InvokeRequest and prints DecisionReceipt + evidence list.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- CLI accepts JSON InvokeRequest and returns DecisionReceipt JSON.
- Every deny includes `reason_code` and evidence ObjectIds.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1r09","title":"[POL] CLI workflow (bundle/diff/preview/apply/rollback)","description":"# [POL] CLI workflow (bundle/diff/preview/apply/rollback)

## Goal
Expose policy bundle workflows via CLI for operators and CI.

## Commands
- `fcp policy bundle create --zone <id> --from <current> --sign`
- `fcp policy bundle diff <old> <new>`
- `fcp policy preview --bundle <id> --samples <file>`
- `fcp policy apply <bundle>` / `fcp policy rollback --to <bundle>`

## Output Requirements
- JSON and human outputs; stable ordering.
- Non-zero exit codes on validation or preview failures.

## Tests
- Unit tests for CLI parsing + output formatting (snapshots).
- Integration tests with test bundles and preview fixtures.
- Structured JSON logs for each stage (create/diff/preview/apply/rollback).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI outputs are deterministic and actionable.
- Rollback is one command and emits an audit event.","notes":"Follow-up: CLI bundle create now uses POLICY_BUNDLE_SIGNED_FIELDS for signed_fields ordering.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ju.2","title":"fcp.mcp-bridge: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

Commit f655652d.","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:04:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7ju.3","title":"TEST: fcp.mcp-bridge Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7sv","title":"fcp.spotify: Playback Control","description":"# fcp.spotify: Playback Control (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe playback control for user devices:
- play/pause
- skip
- seek
- set volume/shuffle/repeat
- transfer playback between devices

Playback control is a real-world side effect and may require Spotify Premium.

## Scope
- Get current playback state and device list.
- Control playback on a specific device.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.playback.read`
- `spotify.playback.control` (dangerous-ish)

Policy guidance:
- Some orgs may require approval for playback control in certain zones.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For control operations:
  - receipts + audit events include device id + action (no track names)
  - handle “Premium required” as a typed denial (not a generic error)

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.playback.get_state`
  - `spotify.playback.devices.list`
  - `spotify.playback.play`
  - `spotify.playback.pause`
  - `spotify.playback.next/previous`
  - `spotify.playback.seek`
  - `spotify.playback.set_volume`
  - `spotify.playback.transfer`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - control request formation
  - premium-required error mapping
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Playback actions are safe, auditable, and do not leak listening content in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-7xm","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Rollout status monitoring and history","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Rollout status monitoring and history (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide deployment rollout visibility so agents can:
- monitor rollout progress
- detect stalled/failed rollouts
- inspect rollout history
- optionally trigger rollback (dangerous)

## Scope
### Read
- Get rollout status for a deployment.
- List rollout history/revisions.

### Write (dangerous; optional)
- Trigger rollback/undo to a previous revision.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.rollout.read`
- `k8s.rollout.rollback` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For rollback:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - emit receipts + audit events with deployment id + revision

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.rollout.status`
  - `k8s.rollout.history`
  - Optional: `k8s.rollout.rollback`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - status parsing
  - watch-based progress updates (bounded)
  - approval gating for rollback

## Acceptance criteria
- Rollout monitoring is reliable and safe-by-default.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support scene discovery and activation for common automation workflows.

## Scope
- List scenes.
- Activate a scene.
- Optional: create/update/delete scenes (dangerous; defer unless needed).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.scenes.read`
- `ha.scenes.activate` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `ha.scenes.write` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Scene activation is a side effect:
  - receipts + audit events include scene id + action

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.scene.list`
  - `ha.scene.activate`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - activate flow
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Scenes can be activated safely and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a0x","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Entity state read and write operations (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core entity state access:
- read current state/attributes
- perform controlled state writes where supported

## Scope
### Read
- Get entity state by entity_id.
- Bulk list states (bounded).

### Write (domain-limited)
- For entities that support direct state writes (e.g., input_* helpers), allow setting state.
- For device control (lights/switches/etc), prefer service calls (`flywheel_connectors-3ef`).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.entities.read`
- `ha.entities.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Sensitive attributes (location, camera URLs, etc.) must be redacted from logs.
- Writes emit receipts + audit events.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.entity.get`
  - `ha.entity.list`
  - `ha.entity.set_state` (domain-limited)

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - state parsing
  - redaction rules
  - write gating
  - receipts/audit for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Entity access is reliable, bounded, and privacy-preserving.

Acceptance: all 6 golden tests and fcp-store repair tests pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SandyOx","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:04:31.034109Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:30:17.346840Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decoder","raptorq","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2033,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak investigation 2026-03-26: Traced the failure path. Encoder produces source ESIs 0..K-1 and repair ESIs K'..K'+R-1 (K' >= K per RFC 6330). Decoder try_reconstruct() classifies received symbols: ESI<K as source (identity equation), ESI>=K as repair (calls repair_equation_rfc6330). Gap K..K' is filled with zero-padded virtual symbols. Basic roundtrip tests PASS (723), only erasure recovery tests FAIL (6 golden + 2 store). encode_store_reconstruct also fails even with all symbols present, suggesting the decoder produces corrupt output under certain symbol count/size combinations. The BTreeMap change (uncommitted at time of testing) is NOT the cause — failures reproduce on the committed HashMap version. Root cause likely in InactivationDecoder Gaussian elimination or the constraint/repair equation wiring. Needs deep codec-level investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:27:35Z"}]}

Encoder produces source ESIs 0..K-1 and repair ESIs K'..K'+R-1 (K' >= K per RFC 6330). Decoder try_reconstruct() classifies received symbols: ESI<K as source (identity equation), ESI>=K as repair (calls repair_equation_rfc6330). Gap K..K' is filled with zero-padded virtual symbols. Basic roundtrip tests PASS (723), only erasure recovery tests FAIL (6 golden + 2 store). encode_store_reconstruct also fails even with all symbols present, suggesting the decoder produces corrupt output under certain symbol count/size combinations. The BTreeMap change (uncommitted at time of testing) is NOT the cause — failures reproduce on the committed HashMap version. Root cause likely in InactivationDecoder Gaussian elimination or the constraint/repair equation wiring. Needs deep codec-level investigation.","created_at":"2026-03-26T22:27:35Z"},{"id":2034,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","author":"SandyOx","text":"Patched two narrow seams for this bead. In `crates/fcp-raptorq/src/decode.rs`, optimistic early decode attempts that hit inner `CorruptDecodedOutput` now map back to outer `InsufficientSymbols` so `add_symbol()` keeps collecting instead of failing closed on mixed source/repair arrival. In `crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs`, `get_all_symbols()` now sorts by ESI for deterministic downstream reconstruction order. Added seam tests for both changes. Next step is guarded verification through `rch`; current blocker is the repo-local `RCH-E326` dependency-closure fail-open behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:39:48Z"},{"id":2035,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a46m4","author":"SandyOx","text":"Guarded verification attempt via `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-raptorq corrupt_decoded_output_maps_to_recoverable_insufficient_symbols -- --nocapture` selected worker `vmi1149989`, then immediately hit `RCH-E326` again: dependency planner refused remote Cargo execution and fail-opened to local. I interrupted it at once with Ctrl-C so local cargo did not proceed. Code patch remains unverified by Cargo because the shared-session rule forbids allowing the local fallback to continue.","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:40:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","title":"fcp.spotify - Spotify Connector (Music & Podcast Platform)","description":"# fcp.spotify - Spotify Music & Podcast Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Plan and implement an FCP2-compliant Spotify connector for playback/library/search workflows with correct OAuth provisioning and strict single-zone isolation.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 72/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (Media/Entertainment family).

**Why Critical**: Spotify is the **largest music streaming platform** (500M+ users). Enables agents to control playback, manage playlists, discover content, and integrate music into productivity workflows. Unique capability for ambient/focus automation.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (Media + Personal Productivity)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 4 Advanced (weeks 13+)

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Playback Control
- Play/pause/skip tracks
- Control volume
- Manage queue
- Device selection

### Library Management
- Search music and podcasts
- Create/edit playlists
- Save to library
- Follow artists

### Discovery & Recommendations
- Get recommendations
- Browse new releases
- Access personalized playlists
- Artist/album exploration

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Play some focus music"
"Add this song to my workout playlist"
"What's currently playing?"
"Skip to the next track"
"Find podcasts about AI"
"Create a playlist for deep work"
"Show me new releases from followed artists"
"Lower the volume to 30%"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Home Assistant Integration
**Scene-based audio:**
- Music as part of home scenes
- Time-based playback automation
- Presence-aware music

### Calendar Integration
- Focus music during work blocks
- Different playlists for meeting types
- Podcast queue for commute times

### Agent Mail Integration
- Music recommendations → agent threads
- Playlist collaboration
- Content discovery sharing

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- Index podcast transcripts
- Search music by mood/genre
- Contextual recommendations

### AI Provider Integration
- Generate playlist descriptions
- Mood analysis for recommendations
- Podcast summarization

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **Web API** — Full Spotify functionality
- **OAuth 2.0** — User authorization required
- **Scopes** — Granular permission model

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `oauth2` — OAuth handling
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Key Scopes
| Scope | Access |
|-------|--------|
| `user-read-playback-state` | Current playback |
| `user-modify-playback-state` | Control playback |
| `playlist-modify-public` | Edit playlists |
| `user-library-read` | Read saved items |
| `user-read-recently-played` | Listening history |

### Resource Model
```
User
├── Playlists
├── Saved Tracks
├── Followed Artists
└── Devices

Track → Album → Artist
Playlist → Tracks
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:media:spotify:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:media:spotify:playback` | Playback only | Control, no library changes |
| `z:media:spotify:readonly` | Read-only | Browse and search |
| `z:media:spotify:playlists` | Playlist management | Playlist CRUD |

### Data Sensitivity
- Listening history reveals preferences
- Playlist names may be personal
- Generally low sensitivity overall

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.spotify.com:443`
- `accounts.spotify.com:443` (OAuth)

### Credential Injection
- OAuth tokens via `CredentialId`
- Refresh tokens for long-lived access
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `spotify.playback.read` — Current playback state
- `spotify.library.read` — Saved items
- `spotify.playlists.read` — User playlists
- `spotify.search` — Search catalog

### Write Operations
- `spotify.playback.control` — Play/pause/skip
- `spotify.queue.add` — Add to queue
- `spotify.playlists.write` — Create/edit playlists
- `spotify.library.write` — Save/remove items

### Administrative
- `spotify.playlists.delete` — Delete playlists
- `spotify.follow.manage` — Follow/unfollow

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Get Current Playback
```json
GET /v1/me/player
```

Response:
```json
{
  "device": {"id": "abc123", "name": "Living Room Speaker"},
  "is_playing": true,
  "item": {
    "name": "Track Name",
    "artists": [{"name": "Artist Name"}],
    "album": {"name": "Album Name"}
  },
  "progress_ms": 45000
}
```

### Start/Resume Playback
```json
PUT /v1/me/player/play
{
  "context_uri": "spotify:playlist:37i9dQZF1DX5trt9i14X7j",
  "offset": {"position": 0}
}
```

### Search
```json
GET /v1/search?q=focus+music&type=playlist&limit=10
```

### Create Playlist
```json
POST /v1/users/{user_id}/playlists
{
  "name": "Deep Work Focus",
  "description": "Music for concentration",
  "public": false
}
```

### Add to Playlist
```json
POST /v1/playlists/{playlist_id}/tracks
{
  "uris": ["spotify:track:abc123", "spotify:track:def456"]
}
```

---

## Productivity Patterns

### Focus Music Automation
```
Agent receives "starting focus session"
→ Search for "focus" playlists
→ Start playback on preferred device
→ Set appropriate volume
→ Queue additional tracks
```

### Context-Aware Playback
- Morning: Energetic playlist
- Work: Focus/concentration music
- Break: Upbeat/casual
- Evening: Relaxing/ambient

### Podcast Integration
- Queue podcasts for commute
- Resume where left off
- Track listening progress

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- OAuth flow handling
- Search query construction
- Playback command formatting
- Playlist operations

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Multi-device scenarios
- OAuth refresh handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Scope enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Spotify calls in unit tests
- Playback control works
- Search functional
- Playlist CRUD operational
- Device selection works
- OAuth refresh implemented

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.1","title":"fcp.spotify: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the Spotify connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** under FCP2.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (search/browse metadata)
  - `operational` (library mgmt, playback control)
  - `streaming` (player state as an event stream; polling-backed)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin Spotify Web API host(s)
  - explicitly pin any image/media download hosts if used
- State model declaration:
  - OAuth refresh state (stored by platform, not connector)
  - player polling cursor/state (if persisted)

## Acceptance criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.2","title":"fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)

## Goal
Automate Spotify onboarding with minimal human steps while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE via provisioning recipes.
- Scope selection:
  - read-only scopes for search/history
  - separate scopes/capabilities for playback control and library writes
- Device readiness checks:
  - verify the account has at least one available playback device (when required)
  - handle “Premium required” cases explicitly
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Mockable in CI.
